input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Critical Fasteners/Critical Fastener`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Critical Fasteners/Critical Fastener` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.3.1.10
            TITLE: fasteners in the brake system are critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.1.10: Fasteners in the Brake System are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
T.3.1.10 Fasteners in the Brake System are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: v.3.2.8
            TITLE: fasteners in the steering system are critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.2.8: Fasteners in the steering system are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
V.3.2.8 Fasteners in the steering system are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: f.5.4.2
            TITLE: threaded fasteners used in primary structure are critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.4.2: Threaded fasteners used in Primary Structure are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
F.5.4.2 Threaded fasteners used in Primary Structure are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: f.10.5.3
            TITLE: any fasteners used to attach accumulator container(s) are critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.5.3: Any fasteners used to attach Accumulator Container(s) are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
F.10.5.3 Any fasteners used to attach Accumulator Container(s) are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: v.3.1.4
            TITLE: fasteners in the suspension system are  critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.1.4: Fasteners in the Suspension system are  Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
V.3.1.4 Fasteners in the Suspension system are  Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: t.8.1
            TITLE: critical fasteners

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.1: Critical Fasteners
T.8.1 Critical Fasteners 
A fastener (bolt, screw, pin, etc) used in a location specified in the applicable rule

RULE: t.8.2.5
            TITLE: some critical fastener applications have additional requirements that are provided in the

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.2.5: Some Critical Fastener applications have additional requirements that are provided in the
T.8.2.5 Some Critical Fastener applications have additional requirements that are provided in the 
applicable section.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 72 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.8.2.1
            TITLE: any critical fastener must meet, at minimum, one of the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.2.1: Any Critical Fastener must meet, at minimum, one of the following:
T.8.2.1 Any Critical Fastener must meet, at minimum, one of the following: 
a. SAE Grade 5 
b. Metric Class 8.8 
c. AN/MS Specifications 
d. Equivalent to or better than above, as approved by a Rules Question or at Technical 
Inspection

RULE: t.8.2.3
            TITLE: all critical fasteners must be secured from unintentional loosening with positive locking

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.2.3: All Critical Fasteners must be secured from unintentional loosening with Positive Locking
T.8.2.3 All Critical Fasteners must be secured from unintentional loosening with Positive Locking 
Mechanisms see T.8.3

RULE: t.5.2.9
            TITLE: attachment fasteners - all fasteners attaching scatter shields and guards must be 6 mm or

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.9: Attachment Fasteners - All fasteners attaching scatter shields and guards must be 6 mm or
T.5.2.9 Attachment Fasteners - All fasteners attaching scatter shields and guards must be 6 mm or 
1/4” minimum diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: t.8.2.2
            TITLE: all threaded critical fasteners must be one of the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.2.2: All threaded Critical Fasteners must be one of the following:
T.8.2.2 All threaded Critical Fasteners must be one of the following: 
• Hex head  
• Hexagonal recessed drive (Socket Head Cap Screws or Allen screws/bolts)

RULE: f.7.8.5
            TITLE: each attachment point requires no less than two 8 mm or 5/16” minimum diameter critical

            CONTENT:
            rule F.7.8.5: Each attachment point requires no less than two 8 mm or 5/16” minimum diameter Critical
F.7.8.5 Each attachment point requires no less than two 8 mm or 5/16” minimum diameter Critical 
Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: f.5.13.1
            TITLE: where the braces are not welded to steel frame members, the braces must be securely

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.13.1: Where the braces are not welded to steel Frame Members, the braces must be securely
F.5.13.1 Where the braces are not welded to steel Frame Members, the braces must be securely 
attached to the Frame using 8 mm or 5/16” minimum diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: f.7.8.7
            TITLE: the front hoop bracing, main hoop bracing and main hoop bracing supports may use only

            CONTENT:
            rule F.7.8.7: The Front Hoop Bracing, Main Hoop Bracing and Main Hoop Bracing Supports may use only
F.7.8.7 The Front Hoop Bracing, Main Hoop Bracing and Main Hoop Bracing Supports may use only 
one 10 mm or 3/8” minimum diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2 as an alternative to F.7.8.5 
above if the bolt is on the centerline of the bracing tube to prevent loading the bolt in 
bending, similar to the figure below.

RULE: f.5.12.1
            TITLE: when roll hoop bracing is mechanically attached:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.12.1: When Roll Hoop Bracing is mechanically attached:
F.5.12.1 When Roll Hoop Bracing is mechanically attached:  
a. The threaded fasteners used to secure non permanent joints are Critical Fasteners, see 
T.8.2.  Additional requirements apply in F.5.12.5 and F.5.12.7 
b. No spherical rod ends are allowed.  
c. The attachment holes in the lugs, the attached bracing and the sleeves and tubes must 
be a close fit with the pin or bolt.

RULE: t.2.6.4
            TITLE: any bolt used to attach a shoulder belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.6.4: Any bolt used to attach a Shoulder Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is
T.2.6.4 Any bolt used to attach a Shoulder Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is 
a Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of: 
• The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer 
• 10 mm or 3/8”

RULE: t.2.5.8
            TITLE: any bolt used to attach a lap belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.5.8: Any bolt used to attach a Lap Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a
T.2.5.8 Any bolt used to attach a Lap Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate bracket, is a 
Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of: 
• The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer 
• 10 mm or 3/8”

RULE: t.2.7.5
            TITLE: any bolt used to attach an anti-submarine belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.7.5: Any bolt used to attach an Anti-Submarine Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate
T.2.7.5 Any bolt used to attach an Anti-Submarine Belt, directly to the chassis or to an intermediate 
bracket, is a Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2, with a minimum diameter that is the smaller of: 
• The bolt diameter specified by the manufacturer 
• 8 mm or 5/16”

RULE: ic.6.2.4
            TITLE: fuel rail mounting requirements:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.6.2.4: Fuel rail mounting requirements:
IC.6.2.4 Fuel rail mounting requirements: 
a. The fuel rail must be securely attached to the engine block or cylinder head with brackets 
and mechanical fasteners.  Hose clamps, plastic ties, or safety wires do not meet this 
requirement.  
b. The fastening method must be sufficient to hold the fuel rail in place with the maximum 
regulated pressure acting on the injector internals and neglecting any assistance from 
cylinder pressure acting on the injector tip.  
c. Threaded fasteners used to secure the fuel rail are  Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: t.2.4.4
            TITLE: attachment of tabs or brackets must meet the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.4.4: Attachment of tabs or brackets must meet the following:
T.2.4.4 Attachment of tabs or brackets must meet the following: 
a. Where brackets are fastened to the chassis, no less than two 6 mm or 1/4” minimum 
diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2 or stronger must be used to attach the bracket to 
the chassis. 
b. Where a single shear tab is welded to the chassis, the tab to tube welding must be on 
both sides of the base of the tab. 
Double shear attachments are preferred.  Tabs and brackets for double shear mounts should 
be welded on both sides.
